entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 2 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: Sydney Morning Herald: 2 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-02 15:08PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • ‘There was provocation’: Wallabies to consider fighting Swain red card
  • Australian boxer Jai Opetaia overcomes broken jaw to become world champion
  • The latest illustrations from artist Matt Golding
  • The latest illustrations from artist Matt Davidson
  • Best of cartoons, July 3, 2022
  • Best of cartoons, July 2, 2022
  • As it happened: Pies pip Suns in another thriller, Dons upset Swans, Cats pulverise Kangas, Dees ease past plucky Crows
  • Test rugby action as it happened: Wallabies defeat England 30-28 after red card and Quade Cooper injury drama
  • Sixes, fours and extras: Broad bowls 35-run Test over after Bumrah blitz
  • Ash Barty, Lance Franklin and Uncle Jack Charles among winners of the 2022 NAIDOC Awards
  • Jones claims referee helped 14-man Wallabies after drought-breaking win over England
  • Porecki's emotional interview with dad
  • England pest sets trap for Swain
  • Golden finish: Six on the trot for Collingwood, and up to the sixth
  • Geelong steamroll hapless North Melbourne to inflict another thrashing
  • First case of diphtheria detected in NSW this century
  • Samu's dazzling try seals famous win
  • Mitchell withdraws from Origin contention
  • NRL Highlights: Rabbitohs v Eels - Round 16
  • Broad hammered in most expensive Test over ever
  • Around the world in 40 days: The PM and top ministers’ overseas blitz
  • Motorists should assume petrol price relief ends in September, Chalmers says
  • ‘He’s not to blame’: Macron meets Albanese to forge fresh Franco-Australian ties
  • Sydney’s south-west told to evacuate as ADF, helicopters ready for flood rescues
  • Balmain’s 150-year-old pub to transform into sports, whisky bar
  • Sydney on high alert for flooding after three rescued amid heavy rain
  • Sydneysiders rally for abortion rights after Roe v Wade shock
  • Staff shortages and school holidays spark chaos at Sydney Airport
  • ‘Heartbreaking’: Beekeepers count their losses as mite threat spreads to Sydney
  • Perrottet’s plan to mobilise the Lismore ‘tinnie army’ for future catastrophes
  • Nikki clung to the gutter in rising floodwater for hours. A kayaker was her only hope for survival
  • ‘Reimagining the college’: One of Sydney’s oldest Catholic schools considers co-ed move
  • Far from being done with COVID, UK records 2.3m cases in a week
  • The sun shines again on the French-Australian relationship
  • Julian Assange appeals to High Court against extradition order
  • ‘A political pawn’: US basketballer Brittney Griner on trial in Russia
  • Firefighters control blazes threatening Machu Picchu
  • ‘Diplomacy, diplomacy, diplomacy’: Ardern on preventing Chinese takeover of Taiwan
  • Biden weighs up using federal sites for abortion care as legal challenges mount
  • Russian missile hits Odesa after Ukraine retakes Snake Island
  • Supreme Court marches US back in time
  • Wildfire in Spain forces evacuation of hundreds of tourists
  • Warm welcome to Paris for Anthony Albanese
  • Petrol prices predicted to increase
  • NSW communities put on alert as rain bomb dropped across state
  • Police investigate death of man found unconscious on freeway
  • ‘Appalling culture’: Private rehab DayHab investigated for trading insolvent
  • Gallery: The best photos from around the world
  • Australia braces for grim COVID-19 milestone
  • Rainfall intensifies across NSW
  • Football community pauses to remember Neil Kerley
  • Thousands descend on Melbourne in protest of US abortion ruling
  • July rain records broken in Queensland
  • Redlands Mayor charged with drink driving
  • Extreme weather and holiday travel sparks delays at Sydney Airport
  • 'Be ready to evacuate': Residents warned 'worst yet to come'
  • Thousands descend on Sydney, Melbourne in protest of US abortion ruling
  • Nuix and Macquarie’s dirty laundry aired in sizzling affidavit
  • ‘Suffocatingly safe’: Where are all the young Australian comedians on TV?
  • GameSir X3 controller review
  • National weather forecast for Saturday July 02

Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: Guardian: 2 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Tour de France 2022: stage two – live!
  • Russia-Ukraine war: Zelenskiy accuses Russia of ‘deliberate terror’; UK condemns ‘exploitation’ of captured Britons – latest updates
  • England v India: fifth Test, day two – live!
  • Wimbledon 2022: Swiatek v Cornet, De Minaur beats Broady, Gauff out – live!
  • Pride in London 2022: huge turnout at first march since pandemic – live updates
  • Libyan protesters set fire to parliament building in Tobruk
  • A nose for history: academics recreate lost smells from the past
  • UK Foreign Office condemns Russian ‘exploitation’ of prisoners in Ukraine
  • Texas supreme court blocks order that allowed abortions to resume
  • ‘Kiwi can’t defend themselves’: New Zealand wrestles with how to save an icon
  • Great auks and seal-headed men: a window into ice age Provence
  • On my radar: Bolu Babalola’s cultural highlights
  • Living in a tree is the only way to save it from pointless destruction | Tim Adams
  • Minions: The Rise of Gru review – very funny goofball nonsense
  • Dozens feared dead as ship sinks in South China Sea
  • Three police die in Kentucky shooting while serving domestic violence warrant
  • Hugo Speer sacked from Full Monty reboot over ‘inappropriate conduct’ claims
  • Women accused of illegal abortions in England and Wales after miscarriages and stillbirths
  • Ekow Eshun: ‘When I grew up I wanted to be Spider-Man’
  • Lorde review – a magnificent night out
  • One to watch: NoSo
  • Summer reading: the 30 best holiday reads – chosen by authors and critics
  • Record crowds, star players, prime-time coverage: Women’s Euro 2022 expects to win big
  • Utah Jazz trade Rudy Gobert to Timberwolves for four first-round picks
  • Pete Samu seals victory as 14-man Australia punish wasteful England
  • Ryanair boss says travellers face years of flight chaos
  • Neon White review – an exhilarating speedrun through a celestial dreamscape
  • Women’s Euro 2022 team guide No 12: Switzerland
  • Taking on Putin through porn: how Russians are finding out the truth about Ukraine | Jemimah Steinfeld
  • Worried about holiday travel chaos? Make sure you have the right insurance
  • ‘Grand responsibility’: How Checo Pérez became Mexico’s sporting hero
  • Original Observer Photography
  • Jerry Hall files for divorce from Rupert Murdoch in US court
  • Sexton injury adds to Ireland woes in crushing defeat by New Zealand
  • Meera Sodha’s vegan recipe for Thai-style pineapple fried rice | The new vegan
  • Pearl Harbor water poisoning: US military families say they continue to fall ill
  • Hidden Germany: where to stay and what to do off the beaten track
  • Father John Misty: ‘Which living person do I most despise? Oh, he knows’
  • From fish curry to lamingtons: Yotam Ottolenghi’s coconut recipes
  • Venice day-trippers will have to make reservations and pay fee
  • I reject the US abortion ruling. I vow to defend the sovereignty of women’s bodies | V (formerly Eve Ensler)
  • ‘My “sad girl” fans concern me’: Ottessa Moshfegh in conversation with Carmen Maria Machado
  • EU’s electric vehicle drive leaves supercars at the back of the grid
  • ‘It felt like history itself’ – 48 protest photographs that changed the world
  • Denmark Vesey’s Bible review: searing history with lessons for a troubled America
  • ‘Perfect storm’ of crises is widening global inequality, says UN chief
  • What links Salvador Dalí to Christopher Wren? The Saturday quiz
  • ‘We want it to come alive’: architect’s plan to transform Notre Dame area
  • Alarm as US supreme court takes a hatchet to church-state separation
  • Blind date: ‘I left his place the following morning, so you fill in the blanks’
  • Iran earthquakes: at least five killed and village flattened, state media says
  • What happened in the Russia-Ukraine war this week? Catch up with the must-read news and analysis
  • ‘Better than we dared imagine’: Sydney Opera House unveils its ‘miracle’ new concert hall
  • Papua New Guinea to head to polls amid election violence fears
  • ‘Free healthcare, bad Mexican food’: what life is really like for US expats in New Zealand
  • The week around the world in 20 pictures
  • Ukrainian borscht recognised by Unesco with entry on to safeguarding list
  • The Guardian view on Leonard Cohen’s Hallelujah: praise be | Editorial
  • Mark Meadows’ associate threatened ex-White House aide before her testimony
Latest international news, sport and comment from the Guardian

entry.title: Washington Post: 2 Jul, 2022
entry.content:

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 2 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 2 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-02 14:11PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Xi Jinping’s meet-and-greet with Hong Kong’s elite set tongues wagging on pecking order of political big guns and second-generation tycoons
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong reports 2,227 infections even as bad weather forces testing centres to close, while Macau confirms 56 cases as outbreak persists
  • From egg tarts to the Star Ferry: 25 objects that tell us this is Hong Kong
  • Xi Jinping’s speech at 25th anniversary of handover ceremony is ‘most expansive’ account of ‘one country, two systems’ and a ‘calming pill’ for doubters: Beijing official
  • How Hong Kong’s unique strengths can help it find success for the next 25 years
  • 7 top innovations President Xi Jinping saw at Hong Kong Science Park
  • How climate change and overfishing drove this Hong Kong property manager to launch a cell-grown seafood business
  • We’ve all had enough of Hong Kong’s housing crisis – break the city’s ‘shoebox curse’ or people will leave and never return
  • 25 years after handover, mainland firms’ presence in Hong Kong grows exponentially and professionals who call city home say ‘the longer you stay here, the more you fancy it’
  • Xi Jinping was talking to Hong Kong but also had a message for the world: the city will continue to enjoy its unique status, remain free and open, analysts say
  • PLA missile interceptor test seen as response to warning from Taipei
  • Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi seeks to bolster Beijing’s position in Southeast Asia on latest tour of region
  • China accuses NSA of FoxAcid hack attack on science research groups
  • China eases Covid entry restriction to allow transit via third country
  • Meet the Chinese fish robots that can ‘eat’ microplastics – and maybe help clean up oceans
  • Hong Kong at 25: where mainland ‘drifters’ have made their home
  • Mystery over purpose of China Aerospace Science and Industry Corporation’s new lab
  • China calls on regional bloc to ‘oppose hegemonic bullying’ as Nato set to expand
  • China’s internet watchdog ramps up crackdown on online scams amid growing number of counterfeit financial services apps
  • Airbus sells 292 A320 aircraft to four Chinese airlines in a blow to Boeing, as US-China tension tips balance in European maker’s favour
  • Don’t interfere in Hong Kong affairs, China tells Canada
  • New Chinese destroyer Lhasa circles Japan in first far-sea training run
  • EU seeks to boost ties with India as relations with China cool
  • China’s Great Wall Motor shelves US$1 billion India plan: sources
  • Blockchain with ‘Chinese characteristics’ quietly takes off with Beijing’s blessing while shunning cryptocurrency
  • Xi Jinping in Hong Kong: what the Chinese president’s entourage tells us about Beijing’s thinking about the city
  • US user data is safe, TikTok CEO tells senators, amid fresh scrutiny over China tech giant’s access
  • Malaysia’s Mahathir proud Malaysia-born Australian is Canberra’s foreign minister
  • Roe v Wade: Indian women fear ‘ripple effect’ after US abortion law struck down
  • Rock star Randy Bachman of the Guess Who, and Bachman-Turner Overdrive reunited with stolen guitar in Japan after 45 years
  • Will Russia ‘punish’ Singapore, Japan and others with cyberattacks, trade restrictions?
  • India, the world’s largest plastics polluter begins single-use ban on cups, straws, and spoons – but not water bottles
  • Why crypto fans in Singapore, Malaysia and elsewhere are upbeat despite the crash and looming regulation
  • Does Xi’s Hong Kong trip mean Chinese leaders are moving past Covid, returning to global scene?
  • Trump documentary maker ‘has armed guards for protection’ after giving evidence to January 6 committee
  • US: Millions of bees escape on Utah road after truck crash; driver, officers stung
  • Musk breaks longest Twitter silence in four years when he meets Pope Francis
  • Ukraine war: blasts rock Mykolayiv after Russian missiles kill 21 near Odesa
  • New York to ban guns in most public places, including Times Square, blocking US Supreme Court ruling
  • WikiLeaks’ Julian Assange appeals to UK court against US extradition
  • Ukraine accuses Russia of dropping phosphorus bombs on Snake Island after retreat
  • Jailed Paralympics star Oscar Pistorius meets murdered girlfriend’s father
  • Ukraine hails victory over Russia in ‘borscht war’ after Unesco ruling
  • Whose world? What order? Time has passed for the West to call the shots
  • WHO calls for urgent action as monkeypox cases triple in Europe
  • China’s economy set to rebound as pandemic risks ease
  • Developing Asian countries shouldn’t have to choose between Western and Chinese investment – not when every dollar helps
  • How to sleep well: have a dog in your room, share your bed with a partner (but not the dog). And amid the pandemic, expect weird dreams
  • The hyaluron pen, a TikTok fave, gives you fuller lips at home – but experts warn it’s not safe
  • Netflix K-drama Cafe Minamdang: lively shaman-themed drama series starring Seo In-guk and Oh Yeon-seo is hit and miss
  • From communist relic to retro chic, the Trabant, ‘freedom car’ East Germans waited years to buy, is a bit of beast to drive, as we discover on a tour of Dresden
  • For Asian Latinos, pride and a unique cultural identity but also loneliness and confusion
  • Simu Liu on growing up poor in China, fights with his parents in Canada and his path to portraying Shang-Chi, Marvel Studios’ first Asian superhero
  • Top Gun’s Tom Cruise at 60 – a look back at his life, from adopting Scientology to his marriages to Nicole Kidman and Katie Holmes and other defining moments
  • Who is Korean billionaire Thai Lee, the sixth-richest self-made woman in America? The Thai-born CEO of SHI International followed Buddhist wisdom to amass a US$4.1 billion fortune
  • 21 mirror-image female celebrities who could be twins: from Millie Bobby Brown’s resemblance to a young Natalie Portman, to Amy Adams and Isla Fisher, plus Penelope Cruz and Salma Hayek’s famous sisterly bond
  • Inside Rob Kardashian’s impressive net worth: he now famously avoids the spotlight, but Kim’s little brother still has millions to his name ... and a California mansion courtesy of mum Kris
  • Was Michael Jackson and Lisa Marie Presley’s marriage real? 19 years after Elvis’ only daughter saw The Jackson 5 perform, the pair eloped – but what did Donald Trump have to do with their romance?
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Financial Times: 2 Jul, 2022

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
